entry.title: Subscriptions in market's secondary currency from solution order - integration with S4hpc
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

for our customer we implemented the standard integration S4hana public cloud <-> Subscription billing.
In Subscription billing, all the markets have EUR as primary currency and USD as secondary currency.
To be able to create subscriptions in both currencies, I followed the instructions of the sap help (https://help.sap.com/docs/subscription-billing/pricing-configuration/multicurrency-pricing), so after assigning both currencies to the markets, I checked the box "multicurrency" in the lookup tables, finally I created pricing records for both currencies in lookup table data.

When I create the subscription directly in Subscription billing, I manually select the currency and I chose the secondary currency USD: in this case, the existing pricing record in USD is correctly determined, the bill is created in USD dollars as well as the billing document request in S4 -> this is the expected result.

The result is different when the process starts from the Solution order.
In the solution order, I select USD as currency of the header of the solution order. When I go at subscription billing item level and I trigger the simulation, in section Price calculation I see that the price in EUR is imported plus a price in USD is also visible but it doesn't correspond to the price record I created in Subscription billing therefore I assume it is a conversion of the EUR price based on the exchange rate stored in S4 (see screenshot attached: In subscription billing I created price 51.77 USD and in the solution order item 54.12 USD is displayed).
When I release the subscription item, the subscription created in Subscription billing is set back to currency EUR. Only If I manually change the currency of the subscription to USD, the price record existing in USD is picked up.
But it doesn't seem efficient at all to have to always go to the follow-up subscription to manually change the currency if we have to manage a high volume of subscriptions, this increase the risk of errors.
It is also not logic to me that an important transactional data such as the currency is not copied to the follow-up transaction making the entire process incoherent.

Do you have any advise/best practice to correctly realize the scenario I described - creation of a subscription in the market's secondary currency from the solution order?

Thank you in advance for the help.

entry.title: Re: Optimizing Production Order Process: Leveraging SAP BTP Event Mesh for Decoupling Inbound Scenar
entry.content:
Actually, we did not need any sequencing. The dead queue receives messages if there is an API lock and frequency, and the volume is low as we have decoupled the process in S4. If you think about the messages, these are transactions, and they posted in sequence in S4 HANA. 

Hope this helps!

Regards 

HS

There are several scenarios to prevent the sales order from being considered after running the MRP 

1. At the sales order creation stage you have to maintain schedule line category CN Then the system will not consider the sales order at the time of MRP 

2. At requirement type & requirement class level you have to uncheck the transfer of requirement check box

entry.title: Re: Should we take the backup of IBP Test & IBP Productio...
entry.content:
Saranya,

Changing time profile is a touchy task, following cases may apply

1- you want extending periods in future or/and remove past ones. This is easy, you need to create the new periods with new periodIDs, and leave unchanged the periodID of periods you keep. The ones you delete will simply disappear if they are not in the uploaded and changed timeprofile. May have to run a purge job I think

2- You want to redefine the Start and End date of existing periods. Don't do you will lose all data for sure. 

3- You simply want to change labels or period weight factors or any associated attributes of periods, just do. Think that your disag logic could be impacted by that and you may need to rebalance some disag from scratch

Anyway for case  and  2 and 3 I would backup, or discuss with SAP as they perform backup checkpoint regularly.

And because SAP is SAP, make a mini test on a separate area just to be sure

 

Cheers, Daniel

entry.title: Re: Sending files from different folders/directories at a...
entry.content: File naming • If you know the exact file names (ABC123.xml and DEF123.xml), you can use this pattern ABC.xml|DEF.xml If the files have common prefixes (e.g., ABC and DEF): ABC*.xml|DEF*.xml If you want to match specific patterns, such as files containing ABC or DEF anywhere in the name: .*ABC.*\.xml|.*DEF.*\.xml .* matches any characters before or after ABC/DEF and \.xml ensures the files end with .xml

entry.title: Re: Using Field Tab in matchcode F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQ...
entry.content:
It's because of the DFIES-OFFSET field which you have left zero, so all your fields take the value at offset 0 which is '200'.

It's complex to calculate the offsets yourself.

It's easier to use the callback subroutine, e.g. here to change CARRID header to 'The airline company':

PARAMETERS p_carr TYPE sflight-carrid.
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_carr.
  SELECT *
    FROM sflight
    UP TO 10 ROWS
    INTO TABLE @DATA(flights).
  CALL FUNCTION 'F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST'
    EXPORTING  retfield         = 'CARRID'
               dynpprog         = sy-repid
               dynpnr           = sy-dynnr
               dynprofield      = 'P_CARR'
               value_org        = 'S'
               callback_program = sy-repid
               callback_form    = 'F4CALLBACK'
    TABLES     value_tab        = flights
    EXCEPTIONS parameter_error  = 1
               no_values_found  = 2
               OTHERS           = 3.
FORM f4callback TABLES   record_tab  STRUCTURE seahlpres
                CHANGING shlp        TYPE shlp_descr
                         callcontrol LIKE ddshf4ctrl.
  LOOP AT VALUE ddfields( ( lfieldname = 'CARRID' reptext = 'The airline company' ) ) REFERENCE INTO DATA(new_ddfield).
    DATA(ddfield) = REF #( shlp-fielddescr[ lfieldname = new_ddfield->lfieldname ] OPTIONAL ).
    ddfield->reptext   = new_ddfield->reptext.
    ddfield->scrtext_s = ''.
    ddfield->scrtext_m = ''.
    ddfield->scrtext_l = ''.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: Precise Control of Fiori App Access with the App Authorization Variants
entry.content:
Introduction

On January 17, 2025, my colleague Christian Hockwarth officially announced the availability of App Authorization Variants in his blog SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502: Flexible Business Role Design with App Authorization Variants.  This new feature gives our customers a highly desirable capability to precisely control individual Fiori App access; one step up from past shortcoming of one business catalog grants access to multiple Fiori apps.

This blog is going to give an in-depth discussion on this new feature on a 2502 Release system of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

Background

Within SAP’s Identity Authorization Management (IAM), user access to transactions or Fiori apps are controlled by the Business User Roles, such as Purchase Manager. Between Business User Roles and apps, there is one layer called Business Catalogs.  You can view Business User Roles as an umbrella supported by one or more Business Catalogs. The real access control power is at these Business Catalogs as illustrated in below figure: when a user has been assigned the Business User Role with Business Catalog 1, he/she gets all access to four Apps, 1 through 4, regardless the App 2 might not be necessary for the user’s job description.

Business Roles, Business Catalogs and Fiori Apps

By taking the business persona into consideration, we give certain business catalogs access to more than one app for the convenience of business users.  For example, the Business Catalog Master Data - Business Partner Display grant access to 20 applications, can display customer data in accounting area as well as in sales area. This thinking is valid when we targeted small and medium enterprises for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition several years ago.

Business Catalog Master Data - Business Partner Display Grants Access to 20 Apps

Fast forward to today’s environment, this one-to-many (one business catalog to multiple Fiori apps) access control thinking shows its limitation, especially when we have GDPR in place (don’t expose data to an unintended audience), and more and more medium to large enterprises are embracing the Public Cloud with strict roles and responsibilities.  My customer told me clearly that she wanted a very limited number of apps to one user, all clearly defined by the job role.

This request was voted highly in our Customer Influence Council called Improved Flexibility in Business Role Design. SAP answered the call.

 

App Authorization Variants in a Nutshell

The solution is to add a layer called App Authorization Variants between the Business Catalogs and the Apps.  Each app is controlled by an App Authorization Variant (AAV).  For example, to access App 1, AAV-A is needed.  App 3 was granted access by Business Catalogs 1 and 2. Now you need either AAV-C or AAV-D to access it.

App Authorization Variants between the Business Catalog and the Apps

With this new layer of control, we can easily meet the requirements of our customers in precisely controlling who can access which app.  For example, by removing AAV-B and AAV-C, the user can only access App 1, 3 and 4 as desired. App 3 is granted by the Business Catalog 2, while App 4 is granted from both Business Catalogs.  App 2 can no longer be accessed even it is part of Business Catalog 1.

 Precise App Access Control with the App Authorization Variant

Now let me give more formal introduction of the App Authorization Variant.

The official name of AAV is “IAM apps of the app authorization variant type”.  What does that mean?

IAM Apps are relatively new to our Public Cloud. They are all IAM related for the name’s sake. We divide these apps into eleven types. The App Authorization Variant is one of them, and relevant to this discussion. In total, there are 7,851 IAM Apps as of today.  Among them, 7,709 are App Authorization Variant type. The AAV is the primary user of the IAM Apps for now, at the 2502 Release of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Different Types of IAM Apps

For the sake of convenience, I will use the term App Authorization Variant (AAV) throughout the blog.

Not all business catalogs support AAV yet.  Among 2,195 Business Catalogs, 1,760 of them supports AAV.  That is roughly 80%. It is not bad considering we have so many business catalogs.

1,760 (or 80%) Business Catalogs Support IAM Apps

 

Examples of Using App Authorization Variants

To access the AAV information, we usually start from app Maintain Business Roles. Take SAP delivered business user role template BR_ADMINISTRATOR as an example. This role contains 54 business catalogs, and 100 IAM Apps (all in App Authorization Variant type). All of these AAV’s are active. You can individually select each one of them to deactivate or activate.

IAM Apps in Business User Role Template BR_ADMINISTRATOR

Now let’s take a closer look at transaction F1303, Maintain Business Users. In the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library, it lists five business catalogs.  That means as long as you have one of these five business catalogs in your business role definition, the user can access app Maintain Business Users.

Business Catalogs Associated with App Maintain Business Users

Now I am going to apply AAV to the user management.  To showcase its effects, I use a 2408 Release system.  Because I have two accounts there, one for System Admin (GY) and another one for Testing (TY). The AAV feature was enabled on this 2408 Release system as an Early Adopter.

First, I create a new business user role by copying from SAP role template BR_ADMINISTRATOR, called it ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR. The user TY is assigned to it.  To simplify the user role, I removed all business catalogs but five IAM related ones.

Five Business Catalogs within Business User Role ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR

There are 15 IAM Apps associated with this Business User Role ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR.  Three of them are user management related as highlighted in green.

15 IAM Apps associated with this Business User Role ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR

As the Business User Role ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR is created by copying from a standard SAP template BR_ADMINISTRATOR, all five Launchpad Spaces are copied over.  When user TY logs on to the system, due to removing 49 unrelated business catalogs, four of these Launchpad Spaces are no longer there.  There is only one Administration Launchpad Space visible. There are 11 Pages belong to this Space, only the Identity and Access Management Page has active app tiles listed.  There are no app tiles shown up on other Pages. We are going to focus on three apps surrounded by the green boxes.

The Identity and Access Management Page for User TY

Now I am going to showcase the effects of AAV.

Scenario 1 – Removing App Tile Display Technical Users

Enter the Edit mode of the Business User Role ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR. Select the IAM App Display Technical Users, hit the Deactivate button, the Save button.

Deactivate IAM App Display Technical Users

After refreshing the web browser, user TY only see three app tiles in the Users and Roles section.  The app tile Display Technical Users is invisible.

The Display Technical Users App Is No Longer part of Identity and Access Management Page for User TY

Scenario 2 – Removing User Role Assignment Capability

To assign a user to the business role, we can hit the Add button as below in the app Maintain Business Roles.

Visible Add button in the app Maintain Business Roles

Now I plan to remove this Add privilege from user TY, I can deactivate the IAM App Assign Business Users to Business Roles F1303_22_TRAN.

Deactivated IAM App Assign Business Users to Business Roles F1303_22_TRAN

After refreshing the web browser, user TY only see three app tiles in the Users and Roles section.  The app tile Maintain Business Roles app is still there, as intended.  By entering the app Maintain Business Roles, user TY can still add users to a business role!  Nothing has changed.

Why? Is it a bug of AAV?

The answer is "No".  If we check the IAM App list carefully, we can see that there is another IAM App Assign Business Roles to Business Users F1492_22_TRAN. This IAM App belongs to a different App F1492.  "Assigning a user to a role" and "assigning a role to a user" are the same thing but different sayings.  They are all performed by the same transaction.

Note: Now I understand better that a transaction and a Fiori app are not identical.  There are one or many transactions embedded in a Fiori app.  The same transaction can be embedded in different Apps, such as F1303 and F1492 in this case.

Deactivated IAM Apps Assign Business Users to Business Roles F1303_22_TRAN and Assign Business Roles to Business Users F1492_22_TRAN

After deactivating both IAM Apps, I can no longer edit the users in the app Maintain Business Roles.  The Add and Remove buttons are greyed out.

User TY Cannot Edit Users in Maintain Business Roles App

I am just curious, how about I try to add a role to the user from app Maintain Business User? The Add and Remove button are also greyed out.

User TY Cannot Assign Business Roles in Maintain Business User App

The AAV works as intended.  Bravo!

 

Conclusion

To meet the customer’s business needs in different environment, SAP released a new feature called App Authorization Variants to create a one-to-one access control between a transaction/app and a business user role. The App Authorization Variants become the smallest entity for business user role design.  With this feature, administrators can precisely grant an app/transaction access to a user from a business role.  There won't be any data exposure to unintended users.

 

References

  • Blog: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502: Flexible Business Role Design with App Authorization Variants
  • What’s New 2502: IAM App – App Authorization Variant
  • Blog: User Management in a Nutshell for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition

entry.title: SAP Developer News, January 23rd, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/01/23/sap-developer-news-january-23rd-2025/

Developer Insight Survey

  • Blog Post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/the-2025-sap-developer-survey-is-live-contribute-now/ba-p/13993753
  • Survey: https://sapinsights.eu.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_6AxTBFzfczfuoJg

New Release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI

  • SDK Release Notes https://github.com/SAP/ai-sdk-js/releases/tag/v1.6.0

Code Connect 2025

  • Conference website https://code-connect.dev/
  • Storm Éowyn https://www.bbc.co.uk/weather/articles/cr46z2dv606o
  • My post-news-item run route https://www.strava.com/activities/13430153502

“Wait for an API Call” Step in SAP Build Process Automation

  • Daniel’s blog post on the new process step: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/new-quot-wait-for-api-call-quot-integrates-processes-with-external-systems/ba-p/13992848

New SAP Community interest group - Integration

  • Integration interest group: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration/gh-p/integration

New Certification Prerequisite for SAP Build Low-Code Certification

  • Description: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/sabrina-brueck_sapcertification-sapbuild-lowcodenocode-activity-7287391385630982145-4G-c

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 2025 Developer Insight Survey

1:14 Code Connect 2025

2:32 New Release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI

3:17 “Wait for an API Call” Step in SAP Build Process Automation

4:23 New SAP Community interest group - Integration

4:47 New Certification Prerequisite for SAP Build Low-Code Certification

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for January 23rd, 2025.

[Riley] The annual SAP Developer Insight Survey is live now. This is the sixth annual running of the survey for us. If you've taken it before, you'll see many familiar questions, things that we use to track trends over time. There's also a deeper exploration this year of topics like generative AI for developers and also developer enablement. In the description section below, you're going to do that. to find two links. The first is actually a link to last year's report. If you haven't looked at that yet, I think you'll find the results interesting. The second link, of course, is to this year's survey. It will take about 12 to 15 minutes of your time to complete. The survey is going to be open for about six weeks. At the end of that time, we'll go off and analyze the data and produce a report that we'll share with you. So I look forward to seeing your comments at the point that we do that. In the meantime, dive into the links, explore it, and we appreciate your feedback. It's important because it helps us shape the programs that we use within the community for 2025. Thank you.

[DJ] Good morning. Storm Éowyn is on its way. It's already bitterly cold. It's starting to rain and the winds are gathering. But I've just got time before I got on my run to tell you about the grassroots annual SAP Tech Conference of the year. That is, of course, Code Connect. Happy way again this year, in the usual place, certainly unruped, in Germany, near Walldorf. In the usual time of year, it's the first full week in July, and we have two usual suspect one-day conferences, the awesome recap and the amazing UI5con, and Code Connect is joined this by a new conference HANA TechCon. So we're also planning to run a couple of CodeJams on the Monday, the first day of the Code Connect week. So let us know if you're interested in those too. For those traveling from afar, there's also an early bird ticket application process. Anyway, all the links are in the description. I'm going to be there and I hope to see you there too.

[Thomas] Hey, I wanted to tell you about the new release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI. This is a great tool for JavaScript developers to be able to build your own custom AI orchestration services and interactive applications. In this new 1.6 release, we see the inclusion of image recognition in the orchestration service, as well as screaming. capabilities in the orchestration client. So if your JavaScript developer looking to get started with SAP AI capabilities, be sure to check out this new release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI. AI.

[Danile] SAP Build Process Automation is introducing a new type of process step. Wait for an API call. The step lets you pause a process automation. and then let an external system call an API to resume it. When you add this type of step to your process, you are creating a new trigger. But instead of this trigger creating a new process instance, it merely resumes an existing instance that had already been paused by the step. The idea is to allow process automation to integrate with external systems and let those systems take over part of the process and then signal back to the process instance that it has finished. It seems like it'll open up a world of possible integration scenarios with process automation. I've written a blog on how to use it, step by step, and I will leave a link to the blog post in the description.

[Antonio] Holla, SAP developers. Did you know that there is a new interest group in SAP community which focuses completely on the integration topic? This is our place to share and connect with a collaborative community of integration developers. Now, what are you waiting for? Go and join a new group. I'm including the link in the description.

[Thomas] I'd like to tell you about a new edition into the certification experience, particularly for SAP build low code no code certification. SAP is adding a new hands-on practical portion of the certification experience. This is a free learning course that comes with system access to allow you to execute exercises to get hands-on practical experience. This is part of a new effort by SAP to bring more practical capabilities and testing as part of the certification experience. So check out the links in the show notes for the addition to the certification, the particular certification that's involved, as well as the free experience learning journey that you can take to get ready for that certification.

entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud Stay Certified update
entry.content:
On behalf of the CRM & CX Product Learning CoE team, I am pleased to announce the release of our updated SAP Sales Cloud Stay Certified Program.

Note: This is for the SAP Sales Cloud product (aka Cloud for Customer) and not SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

The  SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP Sales Cloud confirms your proficiency in the core functional and administrative aspects of SAP Sales Cloud, which are essential for the consultant role. This certification demonstrates your comprehensive understanding and advanced technical skills necessary to contribute effectively as a member of an SAP Sales Cloud implementation project team. It is highly recommended as an entry-level certification for this product.

Upon successfully passing this certification, consultants have the opportunity to extend the validity of their certification by an additional year through the Stay Current program, which involves taking an assessment to ensure their skills and knowledge remain up to date with the latest SAP Sales Cloud innovations and best practices.

Our Stay Certified program consists of:

  • Stay Certified SAP Sales Cloud An online course where you can learn about the major enhancements in the product as of the 2408 release to prepare for the assessment to stay certified.
  • An assessment that evaluates your understanding of the latest features. It can be accessed from the ‘My Learning’ area of learning.sap.com

Note: The Stay Certified offer is for subscribed learners on learning.sap.com platform.

entry.title: Mass creation of catalog views within SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Catalog views within SAP Ariba Catalog solution offers an option to control user access to a catalogs based on a set of constraints. Such catalog views are created usually via user interface by navigating into Manage - Core Administration - Catalog Manager - Catalog views - Add Constraint.
Anyway, this approach allows only for creation of one constraint at the time, it does offer an option to create multiple views at the same time. This limitation can be overcomed by mass creation of catalog views via "Import Catalog views" task. Such approach allows for preparation of multiple catalog views within csv file that can be created/edited using Microsoft Excel. To obtain properly formatted csv file that can be used for catalog view creation, user with Customer Catalog Manager permission can navigate to Catalog Manager - Data Import/Export - Export tab - and run the task "Export Catalog views":

This task will export file named CatalogViewExport.csv, this file without any records looks following:

User can now start populating this file with desired catalog views. Individual catalog view record within csv file should be populated with at least following 3 values for proper functioning:

1. Group.UniqueName or CostCenter.UniqueName
2. Subscription.Name (= Catalog subscription) or CommodityCode.UniqueName or Supplier.SystemID
3. FilterType

If catalog view record is going to utilize certain user group, please check via Manager - Core Administration - User Manager - Groups for the UniqueName of the concerned group, this is because UniqueName of group can be sometimes different than the Name of the group and if correct UniqueName of group is not present in csv file, import will throw an error.
For FilterType field, following 4 values are available for use:

1. IfTrueCanSee - this represents filter type "Start with view nothing, show more catalog items to the qualified users"
2. IfFalseCanSee - this represents filter type "Start with view nothing, show more catalog items to the disqualified users"
3. IfTrueCannotSee - this represents filter type "Start with view all, hide some catalog items from qualified users"
4. IfFalseCannotSee - this represents filter type "Start with view all, hide some catalog items from disqualified users"

In the printscreen below is the example of csv file populated with catalog view records for all 4 different FilterTypes:

Here's the interpretation of how those individual catalog view records from printscreen above will actually work:

1. Catalog subscription "SAP Test Catalog" will be visible to users who belong to group "1st_Test_Group"

2. Catalog subscription "SAP Test Catalog" will be visible to users who do not belong to group "2nd_Test_Group"

3. Catalog subscription "SAP Test Catalog" will be not visible to users who belong to group "3rd_Test_Group"

4. Catalog subscription "SAP Test Catalog" will be not visible to users who do not belong to group "4th_Test_Group"

Once csv file is prepared, it can imported into SAP Ariba via "Import Catalog views" with "Load" option (this is default option for this task). Although user interface of this task states "Enter the location of the CustomCatalog.csv file:" above the button for file selection, file name can be anything else, so user can import file named CatalogViewExport.csv as well.
After successful import, newly created catalog view records are listed under Catalog Manager - Catalog Views:

Beside mass creation of Catalog views via "Import Catalog views" it's possible to perform mass deletion of catalog views as well via same import task. Although mass deletion of catalog views is possible also within Catalog Manager - Catalog Views by clicking the checkbox for the catalog views that should be deleted and hitting "Delete Constraint" button, this might be a little time consuming especially when scrolling through long list of catalog views and looking for views that should be deleted. More effective approach is to prepare a csv file containing only catalog views that needs to be deleted and then running the task "Import Catalog views" with option "Deactivate":

To wrap it up - this blog provides quick overview of how to perform mass creation of catalog views within SAP Ariba Catalog solution. Such mass creation is especially useful when multiple catalogs views are needed, moreover csv file with catalog views can be prepared using Microsoft Excel software that allows for effective management of multiple records.

entry.title: Re: Is BusinessObjects Still Relevant in 2025?
entry.content:
Great and insightful article Dallas,

I agree with everything you mentioned in your article.

Lots of users bit the "it's free" bait some software vendors threw and hit a wall when they couldn't share and distribute their work with their collegues unless they paid a hefty price for the entreprise version of the software. Still the enterprise version lacked the scheduling and distribution power that BusinessObjects provides (at no extra cost 😊).

Sure SAP missed the "Dashboard" train in the last few years but it looks like they're catching up with their recent 4.3 service pack and BI2025.

And for those who still underestimate the power of the "Universe", wait until the developers using the other vendors reporting tools change roles or leave the company and see the mess the new developers will be in when they inherit those reports. The ease of adhoc reporting provided by the Universe is unbeatable.

 Looking forward to have fun with the new version.

entry.title: Re: I am unable to figure out the odata response body whe...
entry.content: In a sequential multicast, if any of the branches fail to process a message, the entire flow will fail. This is because the sequential multicast processes each branch one after the other, and an error in any branch will stop the execution and throw an exception.

entry.title: Re: The Relevance of Business Applications in the Agentic Future of AI
entry.content:
And here I need to write again....

Another follow-up.

What did he have for breakfast? LSD?

Microsoft CEO’s STUNNING Reply: "Agents Will REALLY Replace All Software” 

To exemplify how absurd the claim that you will only need agents and a DB, lets use this SAP example. SAP has S/4. It has millions of lines of code of business rules. The value is in the business logic. Agents will be the new UX for sure and ERPs will be used by Agents. Agents will consume existing ERP APIs. So ultimatey yes Agents will be the norm, but saying they would replace ERPs and you will only need a DB is crazy. For example: the release a purchase order, there are thousands and thousands or lines of business rules. Would them be rewritten in an Agent Skill??? No way, makes no sense. They will easily learn how to use the APIs instead.

entry.title: Re: Uploading simulations and a book page with long text...
entry.content:
Hi Helenka,

It sounds to me like that you are trying to generate a Compound Document in Enable Now.  This Compound Document could consist of the content from multiple Book Pages and Simulations. 

As Bart mentions below, you have quite a lot of control over the way that content generates using Enable Now.  For example, you can modify the formatting of the content using Text Styles, the documentation templates located in the Resources > Adaptable Resources > Documentation Style section, and the documentation settings located in the Resources > Documentation Settings area in Producer.  For more information on the setup of your documentation, refer to this video.

Once you configure your documentation settings, you will need to structure your documentation into the order that you want it to generate into the documentation.  So, if you need the content of 3 Book Pages in front of the content of 10 Simulations, add the 3 Book Pages to a folder (Group) object, followed by the 10 Simulations.  Then, click on the folder in the Explorer view of Producer and select Documentation > Generate Compound Documentation.  This will generate a single document containing all the information in the objects that are child objects of your selected folder object.

It is possible to display this generated documentation in a library but it is best if the generated PDF documentation is loaded into Enable Now as Media Objects.  If the documentation is added into your content structure as PDF objects, they will not appear in the content hierarchy of your library.  If you reloaded the generated PDF documents into Enable Now as Media Objects and place them into the library structure, they will appear in the library structure and be easy to access by users of the library.

Good luck!

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: The Way of Clean Code: #1 Single Responsibility et al.
entry.content:
@Jelena_PerfiljevaI was raised on zevolving :). Naimesh was the first one to show me that ABAP is not some relic of past, that it is feasible to employ modern software architecture techniques.

Back to merit: I don't find any issue here. If I select with join, it means that I combine all the data into one table. I probably do it for some report. I imagine that I select some basic data from header, some statuses, and ship-to-party name and address (so it's a partner table + ADRC + ADR6). No matter how big the join is, one line still represents a delivery header. But if I join it with items, then one line represents delivery item. I have one method then: fetch_delivery_items(). There is one big join, or maybe even several selects (e.g. selecting from MAKT should be separated from JOIN to use buffering), but the method does one thing.

In another scenario, where I select header (LIKP + ship-to-party) to one table and items (LIPS + MARA + MAKT) into another, it clearly shows I make 2 things. So here I have:

METHOD fetch_delivery.
  fetch_header().
  fetch_items().
ENDMETHOD.

fetch_delivery() does one thing only (on it's own level of abstraction). fetch_header() and fetch_items() also do one thing only but on the lowest level of abstraction. That's why for me there is no proper appliance of SRP without understanding a concept of abstraction levels.

Principles in development are very, very important if you want to write a good code, but they are not a holy grail. If you create complex enough app, you will end up breaking more than one principle anyway and it's not always that bad. E.g. you will never reach full decoupling, you only try to reach as loose coupling as possible, providing it brings value. If gaining more loose coupling will cost building some complex abstraction structure, maybe it's not worth it. If you write a small classic report, split it just into 2 classes from Naimesh's example: lcl_data_selector for model and lcl_output_alv for view. You don't even need a controller, because body of the report itself will work as one. If the report is simple enough, introducing interfaces just to decouple those two classes is pointless (especially if they are local). Splitting lcl_data_extractor into smaller classes also seems pointless. It's a trade-off and it's a good one. In the future, if the logic becomes complex, you can refactor and split this class.

If you want more examples, we can play in the way that you bring me a scenario, I write how I see the SRP in this scenario and then you criticise for refinement - this way we could build a decent set of good quality examples.

entry.title: Re: Advice on Curriculum Basis Date for clients new globa...
entry.content:
Hi there. I can't answer to your migration part, but wanted to help by providing a little more detail. the basis date has a default when you create a new curriculum. That default is set to 1/1/2010 but we change that all the time, or remove it altogether each time we create a new curriculum. Let me explain.

That default basis date only matters if your initial basis is a CALENDAR based basis. That default date doesn't apply to an EVENT initial basis. So it's a required field only for a calendar basis (whether initial assignment or retraining assignment).  In fact you can delete it on an event initial basis. But all our annual training courses we launch once a year at the same time and due at the same time, are each added to a curriculum with an initial number of 1, period of year, threshold ENTIRE PERIOD, initial basis calendar, required date basis is the assignment date (we schedule an assignment profile), and the basis date is set to the previous year + 1 day. In other words, if our content is required by 9/30/25, then your basis date is 10/1/24 (because the initial basis was one calendar year).

I tend to doubt that all the client's existing curricula in the client's LMS are all the same or that they never changed the basis date, but you would want the migration to pull all the curricula' prior configuration.

entry.title: Re: Implementation of Restrictions for Tyre and Battery I...
entry.content:
Hi mohit,
Let me understand the case first.
So the vehicle is maintained in your system as an equipment.
But the tyres and the batteries - I guess these are Spare Parts, right?
As long as you do not need to track those components separately, do some maintenance on them, track their maintenance history - these are pure consumables, maintained as Spare Parts only.

There is something called iPPE, but for your scenario it will be an overkill.
I believe going for custom development is the way to go.

Personally, I would define a class for the vehicle, something like ZPM_VEH_CONFIG_VAN.
Define characteristics, one of them will be TYRES, second BATTERY etc.
Assign the class to the vehicle, TYRES: value 10, BATTERY: value 1.
Build a custom mapping table, where you classify material numbers of tyres and batteries.
Implement custom logic/development, which gets triggered once those materials are about to be installed. 
The program checks current amount of tyres installed in the equipment and compares it with the class 'configurator'. If it's already 10, an error message shows up.

Please let me know what you think!
Dan

entry.title: EWM Stock Removal -- Full Quantity followed by FIFO
entry.content:
Hello:

Looking for a solution for this scenario of Stock Removal --

  1. If any bin can fill the entire ODO item quantity, use that as the destination bin for the Stock Removal WT. 
  2. If no such bin found, use FIFO Stock Removal strategy

So in scenario below -- if ODO item requires quantity 5 of Product abc -- the destination Bin would be Bin A (since A can fill the entire quantity requested). If the quantity was 7 it would be C followed by B (FIFO) .

 

BinProductQty GR Date
Aabc612/24/2024
Babc412/23/2024
Cabc312/22/2024

 

The idea is that it is more efficient to pick from 1 bin if the entire request can be filled from there. But if that is not possible, use FIFO.

Thanks!

EWM - Delivery Processing EWM - Warehouse Order Processing

entry.title: Re: Text/Association missing in the Text tables of sap da...
entry.content:
After reviewing the examples from SAP's Samples GitHub, where hierarchies are created, I observed the following:

  1. Remote tables are left exactly as they are imported. First, because some of their characteristics cannot be modified, and second, because they might be needed later for other tasks or datasets.
  2. What the examples actually do is create a graphical view using the imported remote table as a source without transformations. In the properties of that view, all the semantic usage configurations for both fields and the object are applied. Additionally, keys that are required and not necessarily defined in the source table can be specified.
  3. Within those same views, if necessary, dimension-type views are associated with text-type views

I also noticed that the views included in the published GitHub packages come without any warnings once they are imported, however, if one tries to recreate them exactly, the warning you mentioned still appears. 

In conclusion, the warning will continue to appear even if the views are created, but you will still be able to change the required properties. For now, the warning does not prevent saving and deploying the object.

I hope this helps.

entry.title: Watch the Replays of Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
GROW with SAP is a comprehensive package combining solutions, services, community support, and learnings to help businesses of all sizes successfully adopt cloud ERP. At its core lies SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, our fully-fledged cloud ERP designed to optimize processes and drive intelligent, sustainable operations. 

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Early Release Series is a biannual event showcasing live sessions led by product experts. These sessions provide an exclusive preview of upcoming innovations, including AI-driven features and advancements with Joule, tailored to selected lines of business and industries. Spanning several days, the series offers expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A, giving participants a first look at exciting updates and the opportunity to get their questions answered directly.

From January 21 to January 24, 2025, we're hosting 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. Don't miss this chance and watch the recordings on demand– anywhere, anytime.

Please note that, as the series is still ongoing, not all recordings have been uploaded to the SAP Learning Hub yet. Thus, resulting in a "no classes available" note when following some of the links.

An active SAP Learning Hub license is required to consume the recordings. Find out how to sign up​

  • As a customer:​ Access to at least the SAP Enterprise Support Edition is required. Follow these steps to sign up:​
    1.  Navigate to the sign-up page and click on "Sign Up".
    2. Authenticate yourself with your S-user ID and password.​
    3. The system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalogue. This process may take up to 2 hours.​
    4. You will receive a registration confirmation via e-mail once the registration process is complete.​
  • As a partner: Partners can access the recordings through their SAP Partner Edge Edition.​
  • As an SAP internal: Register here for the SAP Learning Hub Internal Edition (free of charge).

Experiencing issues? Make sure to be logged on before registering. In case of any other issues, reach out to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Live Product Expert Sessions.

Explore more replays of our past Early Release Series and find out more about our live product expert sessions here.

entry.title: GenAI features self-activation step by step with screenshots
entry.content:
Did you know that system administrators can turn on SAP SuccessFactors GenAI features themselves once 2 simple prerequisites are met ? In this blog we cover prerequisites, RBP steps (with screenshots) and AI Units consumption explanations for a successful start with SAP SuccessFactors GenAI features.

 

Prerequisites

  • Customers must have purchased SKU 8016532 “SAP AI Units” through their Account Executive (SAP internal note: navigate to Cloud Reporting then search for SKU number which should appear if purchased). Customers must have also signed the T&Cs with their AE. Be aware that there are other SKU with a different number that have “AI Units” in their title but only SKU 8016532 will allow GenAI features to work properly.
  • The environment where GenAI is about to be permissioned/activated must have the checkboxes below checked in Provisioning > Company settings (this can only be done by SAP Provisioning teams). SAP internal note: If a few days after purchasing AI Units with SKU 8016532 the below checkboxes still aren’t checked then escalation must be made by email to SF Provisioning team (or reach out to me or anyone from SAP AI Adoption team (Leads: NA: Parul Luthra / EMEA: Jenny Geipel / APJ: Adarsh Mahalingappa). Please also note that the latest version of each module is required for GenAI to work.
    • Enable AI-Assisted SAP SuccessFactors HCM Content Generator
    • Enable AI-Assisted SAP SuccessFactors HCM Skills

 

Step 1

  • Access "Manage Permissions Roles"
  • Grant "Manage AI Capabilities" to Super Admin role

  • Log out / Log back in

 

Step 2

  • In Search for Actions type “AI Services Administration” page then turn on AI features as per screenshot below. If you don’t see “AI Services Administration” in Search for Actions  after several hours then have SAP Support or a certified consultant run a RBP Refresh from Provisioning > Company Settings.

  • Log out / Log back in 

 

Step 3

  • In "Manage Permission Roles" find the "Employee on Self" role (or create a new one for only some employees/testers) then in “User Permissions” select “AI Access”. If you don’t see “AI Access” section even after several hours then have SAP Support or a certified consultant run a RBP Refresh from Provisioning > Company Settings.
  • Grant permissions for the right AI feature by checking the right boxes.

 

  • Log out / Log back in
  • Proxy as an employee who is part of the role you granted AI Access permission for and you can start testing Gen AI features.

 

How to track consumption

  • Consumption dashboards in SAP For Me get updated overnight after the first use of AI features and show AI Units consumption from all environments where Gen AI features are used (any environment with GenAI features being used is billable). The dashboards only show GenAI features consumption of AI Units, not Joule (Joule consumption of AI Units is only available at this time through a report that can be requested by customers to their SAP CSM).
  • More information on the SAP For Me > Business AI dashboards can be found in this video: https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_dahk6ib7
  • If customer is still seeing an error message in the SAP For Me > Business AI dashboard even a few days after activating GenAI features please see this KBA for possible explanations and create an incident to support. 
  • To understand AI Units consumption better, please see in the screenshot below the concept of 1 A4 page = 0.007 AI Units. To find out how many words are considered in an A4 page we advise customers to copy paste a sample job description from their environment into a A4 Word document with standard margins and line spacing = 0 (copy the job description several time until bottom of A4 page is reached) then use the word count feature.

 

  • Joule message consumption – Is it separate than AI Units ? 
    • AI Units are allocated separately as part of Joule vs GenAI activation but their consumption is connected as per example below:
      • As of 1/23/2025 when Joule is activated AI Units are allocated to each customer at no cost through SKU 8017178 based on number of users in the SuccessFactors environment.
      • In order to turn on GenAI features customers must purchase AI Units through SKU 8016532 (prerequisite)
      • If customers that have both Joule and GenAI use all AI Units allocated through SKU 8017178 (Joule) then SAP will take from remaining AI Units allocated through SKU 8017718 (Gen AI) for billing purposes.

 

Additional links: 

  • Artificial Intelligence in SAP Success Factors Guide for Activation –Premium AI Use Cases (ppt presentation)
  • Premium AI Features in SAP SuccessFactors (Implementation guide)
  • SuccessFactors AI - AI-assisted writing and writing - translation (Set up and hints)
  • Unlock the Power of SAP HCM GenAI: Discover Use Cases, Pre-requisites, and RBP Requirements

 

All the best,

Xavier



(If you found this blog useful please consider giving it a Like)

entry.title: Re: Clean and replace subset of data - Measure SAC
entry.content:
Hi N1kh1l,

I have tested this scenario multiple times and it seems that 'clean and replace subset of data' will wipe out the entire facts table within the specified scope of dimensions, irrespective of the ETL mapping of the measures in the import settings.

I.e. Import model consisting of {Dim1, Dim2, Dim3, Dim4} and {Measure1, Measure2, Measure3}

ETL 1 (clean and replace subset of data) - where measures are mapped only for Measure1 and Measure2

ETL 2 (clean and replace subset of data) - where measures are mapped only for Measure3

On both runs, it will wipe out all measures and replace, so the end state after ETL2 is Measure1 and 2 will be dropped.

Do you know if there is a workaround to clean and replace subset ONLY for specific measures?

Regards,
Benji

entry.title: SAP Integration with Concur Solutions becomes available for Concur Standard
entry.content:
Get ready for the next step in intelligent travel and expense management! Starting with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, the SAP Integration with Concur Solutions (a.k.a. ICS a.k.a. native integration) supports a seamless integration with SAP Concur Standard Edition. This native integration offers the benefits of a streamlined integration setup by delivering the next level of automated financial integration and real-time data synchronization for growing businesses using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

For those of you who aren't familiar yet with ICS, let me give you a short overview.

ICS is available for 

  • SAP ECC 6.0 and SAP Concur Professional Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA and SAP Concur Professional Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP Concur Professional Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition with SAP Concur Professional Edition and SAP Concur Standard Edition

While the scope varies for the different ERP and SAP Concur environments, you can integrate such master data like cost objects and transactional data like expense reports and cash advances.

For integrations between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Concur Standard Edition, consider the following:

  • Employee replication using ICS is not available. Instead, you can use the SAP Master Data Integration service to handle the replication of your employee master data in your landscape.
  • The Enhanced WBS Element Search feature is not supported.
  • The Budget Integration feature is not yet supported.
  • Processing payroll documents is not yet supported.

If you got questions/feedback, please leave a comment.

entry.title: SAP Integration with Concur Solutions: Replicating Authorized and Cost Object Approvers
entry.content:
With the S/4HANA 2023 release, the user replication of SAP Integration with Concur Solutions, the standard integration between SAP Concur and SAP S/4HANA a.k.a. ICS a.k.a. Native Integration, has been switched to the User Provisioning (UPS) v4 API. That API allows to replicate a lot more user-/employee-specific data than the previously used User v1, which has been deprecated.

Mid of 2024, the UPS API has been enhanced to support the replication of Authorized and Cost Object Approvers, a long awaited feature. With S/4HANA 2023, feature pack 3, ICS will support that feature end-2-end. I will explain how to set up a simple Cost Object Approver replication to explain the new functionality.

 

Use Case

All expenses are allocated to cost centers. The cost center responsible is supposed to approve all expenses which are allocated to their cost center.

 

How To Configure The Cost Center Approver

Prerequisites:

  • In the SAP Concur system the cost object list must be utilized for the cost object approval to make use of this functionality.
  • Maintain the employee who will be the cost center owner as such in field User Responsible (VERAK_USER) for all cost centers. The linkage between the user record and the employee record is stored in infotype 105 (Communication), sub type 0001 (System User Name), field System ID.

If you call up the field mapping of the employee replication within the transaction Concur Integration Setup (CTE_SETUP), you will see the new section spendApproverLimit. Beneath that section, you will see two more sections: authorizedApprover and costObjectApprover.

New sections for spendApproverLimit, costObjectApprover, and authorizedApprover

 

Press the Create button to the right of the costObjectApprover section. A pop-up will be raised where you need to select the approvalType. Select report for defining the costObjectApprover for an expense report.

Select report as approvalType

A data set for defining the mapping for an expense report's costObjectApprover is created. The approvalType is defaulted to report. Implementation code EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER is defaulted for determining the approvalGroup. 

Implementation Code EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER is defaulted

The implementation code EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER works as follows:

  1. Determine all cost centers where the employee who is supposed to be replicated is maintained as User Responsible (VERAK_USER).
  2. Add a data record to assign the employee as cost center approver to each cost center from Step 1. In the payload that is sent to Concur, you will see the approvalGroup field filled according to the definition for the cost center replication.
    Let's assume that you use the set up which is considered as Best Practice, i.e., Logical System -> Company Code -> Cost Object Type -> Cost Object ID. Then the value in the approvalGroup field will be the concatenation of those 4 values separated by a dash, e.g. QVGCLNT354-1710-CC-SE_301024.

Depending on your Concur set up, you might also have to provide values for the other fields in the costObjectApprover section, e.g. approvalLimit, reimbursementCurrency, level ...

 

What's Missing

So far, we've defined that an employee can be replicated as cost center approver for expense reports. But we haven't defined yet that the employee should also get the related authorization (role Expense Approver, EXP_APPROVER).
The role assignment is done via the section spendRole. If you haven't defined a mapping for the Expense Approver role yet, press the Create button for spendRole and select EXP_APPROVER from the pop-up. 

 

Select Expense Approver role

Now, you can define when the Expense Approver role should be assigned to an employee. To assign the role to an employee that is maintained as User Responsible for a cost center (see also the explanation above for EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER),  pick the implementation code EE_IS_CC_RESPONSIBLE for the field roleName.

Enter EE_IS_CC_RESPONSIBLE as implementation code for the field roleName

Save your changes. From now on, the system will replicate an employee as cost object approver if the employee is the User Responsible for at least 1 cost center.

 

Additional Remarks

  • Authorized approvers are configured in a similar manner as cost object approvers.
  • For determining whether an employee should be assigned an approver role, the implementation code EE_IS_RLU_MGR_OR_CC_RESP will also be available. The implementation code combines the functionalities of EE_IS_RLU_MANAGER and  EE_IS_CC_RESPONSIBLE.
  • The BAdI BADI_CTE_HCM_EE_DATA_CHANGE_V4 has been enhanced to cover spendApproverLimit functionality. For example, you can create your own logic to determine the approvalGroup by implementing the BAdI.

entry.title: Re: Touch points in SD when creating Sales order with TM
entry.content:
The method CL_SD_SLS_TM_INT=>EXECUTE_TM_UPDATE is the final step in LO-INT-TM-SLS. It triggers the method /SCMTMS/CL_SD_TM_INTEGRATION=>EXECUTE_TM_UPDATE, which is the initial step for TM-INT-LI-SD to prepare data integration and FU building.

The planning update from TM FO back to ERP documents is only available for TM-LE delivery update in normal cases. However, if you use the Sales Order Scheduling function, the sales order will be updated with suggested dates from TM. For more details about Sales Order Scheduling, please refer to the following links:
- What's New in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 - Sales Order Scheduling Based on TM Durations
- SAP Blog SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1: TM master data based Sales Order Scheduling

entry.title: Re: Extracting Line Items from the a Document Template
entry.content:
Hello,

You are on the right direction! My personal approach on this would be:

1. Create a document template (in which you have already shown)

 

2. Select the appropriate document type (invoice, po, payment,receipt). In this way you'll automatically have the necessary schema and fields that you wanted to extract. ( I prefer to use the existing and perform the annotation on #3)

3. As soon you select the schema, you can custom the outputs you wanted to extract. At the same time you train on what fields  will be extracted.

4. Technically speaking, you do not need to select all line items in able for DocX to extract everything. Its intelligent to assume where is the starting line item and the last item.

5. The lineItems will become a List may contain a blank lineitem data in the event there was a blank data. 

( I have issues on my trial account at the moment, I can only send limited screenshots.) Feel free to respond, happy to help.

-weisson
